mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20d return volatility in high UNRATE regimes falls below 15%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and declines in the federal funds rate create a 'tight monetary' environment where central bank policy fails to offset inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY and FEDFUNDS declines > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined CPI/FEDFUNDS signal falls below 0.05 over 3-year window, or 20d returns in triggered periods show positive correlation with SPX.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth above 3% annualized in the prior quarter signals robust economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high GDPC1 growth periods fall below 5% annualized, or GDP growth and SPX returns show negative correlation in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict SPX 5d returns with positive Rank IC > 0.10, or 5d returns in high INDPRO periods show negative correlation with SPX.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k in a month signals a tightening labor market, which increases wage pressures and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through inflationary concerns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS periods exceed 3% annualized, or PAYEMS growth and SPX returns show positive correlation in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in residential sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for housing, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral momentum.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 5% in 1 month
failure_condition: Combined RSAFS/HOUST signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year window, or 20d SPX returns in triggered periods fall below 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent CPI inflation above 3% YoY in a high-fed-funds-rate environment signals central bank inaction against inflation, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through real rate increases.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY and FEDFUNDS > 3% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: CPI/FEDFUNDS signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year window, or 60d SPX returns in triggered periods exceed 4% annualized.
